verifier_issues_summary: Could not programmatically parse flow_gating_3.xlsx (openpyxl/xlsxwriter unavailable) so formula-level verification is not directly evidenced; grader appropriately flagged this limitation.

Expert memo preview is truncated in the artifact preview, limiting full-text verification of some claims (the visible preview is consistent with grader reasoning, but the full memo could contain additional details).

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

passed_checks: numeric checks in memo preview (viability, gate_range spread, budget sums) are consistent with artifact previews and trace

verifier_issues_summary: Could not programmatically parse flow_gating_3.xlsx (openpyxl/xlsxwriter unavailable) so formula-level verification is not directly evidenced; grader appropriately flagged this limitation.

Expert memo preview is truncated in the artifact preview, limiting full-text verification of some claims (the visible preview is consistent with grader reasoning, but the full memo could contain additional details).

Grader relied on the expert_memo text preview for formula- and cell-level claims because the XLSX could not be parsed in the grader runtime (openpyxl unavailable). This limits direct, programmatic verification of per-cell formulas.

Minor ambiguous language in the memo about remediation effect direction (e.g., 'improves quality metrics downward (worsens)') introduces semantic clarity issues that reduce confidence in interpretation of some option effects.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

extra_model_fields.recommended_action: Verifier should open the provided XLSX in an environment with openpyxl (or request a formula-view/CSV) to confirm cell-level formulas and resolve minor memo wording ambiguities before full assurance.
extra_model_fields.reproducible_notes: All artifacts are present in actual_outputs and previews. Full reproducibility requires opening flow_gating_3.xlsx in an environment with openpyxl (or providing a CSV/formula-view) so per-cell formulas can be inspected.
